g.data-narration: Welcome to Column-Based NoSQL Systems. We explore databases that organize data by columns for massive scalability and performance.
Wide-column databases store data in columns instead of rows, optimized for analytical queries and large-scale data operations.
g.text: NoSQL databases that store data in

g.data-narration: Tech giants like Google, Facebook, and Amazon created wide-column systems to handle web-scale data processing challenges.
g.text: • Massive datasets with billions of rows/columns

g.data-narration: The fundamental data structure includes Row Key, Column Family, Column Qualifier, Timestamp, and Value - each serving a unique purpose.
g.text: Flexible schema: Different rows can have different columns

g.data-narration: Wide-column databases use column families where each row contains different columns, enabling efficient sparse data storage.
g.text: Not every row needs every column

g.data-narration: Horizontal scalability is achieved through partitioning using consistent hashing or range-based strategies, distributing data across nodes.
g.text: Row key determines data distribution → Choose keys carefully to avoid hot spots

g.data-narration: Wide-column databases offer tunable consistency - developers can choose levels like ONE, QUORUM, or ALL for read/write operations.
Data modeling in wide-column stores is query-driven, using patterns like wide rows, composite keys, and time-series organization.
Wide-column databases use LSM tree storage engines that optimize writes by appending data and periodically merging sorted files.
Apache Cassandra uses CQL for queries. This example shows a social media posts table with composite primary key design.
g.text: • Composite PK (user_id, post_id) stores all posts per user in one partition
• Clustering order DESC returns most recent posts first

g.data-narration: Apache HBase models Google's BigTable. This IoT example uses row key design with sensor_id and timestamp for efficient range queries.
Amazon DynamoDB provides managed wide-column storage with global secondary indexes for flexible query patterns in e-commerce.
Wide-column databases excel in write-heavy workloads and massive scalability but sacrifice ACID transactions for eventual consistency.
Remember: Wide-column stores data by columns, uses LSM trees, offers tunable consistency, and is query-driven like Cassandra CQL.
This concludes our exploration of wide-column NoSQL databases. Practice data modeling and compare with other NoSQL types for exams.
g.text: Apache Cassandra • Apache HBase • Amazon DynamoDB • Query-driven design • LSM Trees • Tunable Consistency
